query: Magnificent 7 tech companies (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) large acquisitions $10 billion or more announced or rumored in 2026, especially mid-2026. Any pending big M&A deals as of June-July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary:

## Bottom Line

**The only confirmed pending Magnificent 7 traditional acquisition of $10B+ as of June–July 2026 is Amazon's ~$11.6 billion acquisition of Globalstar** (announced April 14, 2026), currently in FCC review with a public-comment deadline of July 6, 2026. It's Amazon's second-largest acquisition ever, aimed at boosting its Leo satellite business against Starlink — and it includes Amazon assuming Apple's ~20% Globalstar stake.

**No other confirmed pending $10B+ takeover** by Alphabet, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, or Tesla surfaced. The key structural insight for a forecaster: Mag 7 companies in 2026 are deliberately **avoiding conventional big acquisitions** in favor of:
- **AI infrastructure/cloud contracts** (Meta-CoreWeave $21B, Meta-Nebius $27B)
- **Chip supply deals** (Meta-AMD up to $60B, Tesla-Samsung $16.5B)
- **Structured minority stakes** (Meta-Scale AI $14.3B for a *non-voting* 49% stake; Nvidia-OpenAI up to $100B; Amazon considering ~$10B in OpenAI)

This structuring is a deliberate response to intense FTC/DOJ antitrust scrutiny (including of "reverse acqui-hire" deals like Nvidia-Groq). PitchBook confirms Big Tech acquisition *counts* hit a **decade low** (12 YTD) even as AI capex approaches $600–725B. Notably, the biggest pending M&A deals of 2026 (Netflix-WBD $83B, PIF/Silver Lake-EA $55B) are **not** Mag 7 acquirers, and China blocked Meta's smaller ~$2B Manus deal in April 2026.

key_facts: Amazon announced a definitive agreement to acquire Globalstar for ~$11.6 billion ($90/share cash-and-stock, ~23.5% premium) on April 14, 2026 — its second-largest acquisition ever after Whole Foods ($13.7B, 2017).
The Amazon-Globalstar deal remains PENDING as of July 2026; the FCC opened its review ~June 8, 2026 with a public-comment deadline of July 6, 2026.
Amazon will also assume Apple's ~20% stake in Globalstar as part of the deal; Apple was Globalstar's exclusive iPhone satellite (Emergency SOS) partner.
No other confirmed pending traditional $10B+ takeover by Alphabet, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, or Tesla was found as of early July 2026.
PitchBook (June 4, 2026): Big Five (Google/Microsoft/Amazon/Meta) acquisition count hit a decade low — 12 YTD in 2026 vs 14 in 2025 and 7 in 2024 — while spending ~$600B on AI infrastructure.

Mag 7 large-scale capital deployment in 2026 is overwhelmingly via AI chip/cloud/infrastructure contracts and structured minority stakes, deliberately avoiding full merger review amid FTC/DOJ scrutiny.
Meta invested $14.3B for a 49% NON-VOTING stake in Scale AI (valuing it ~$29B), structured to avoid a full DOJ merger challenge.
Nvidia's ~$20B Groq licensing/reverse-acqui-hire deal (finalized late 2025) is under Senate (Warren/Blumenthal, March 2026) and FTC antitrust scrutiny.
Meta's ~$2-3B acquisition of Chinese startup Manus was blocked/ordered unwound by China's SAMR in April 2026.
The largest pending M&A deals of 2026 (Netflix-Warner Bros Discovery ~$83B; PIF/Silver Lake-Electronic Arts ~$55B; Charter-Cox $34.5B) are NOT Magnificent 7 acquirers.
xAI's ~$60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor) in June 2026 is Musk-affiliated but xAI is not a Magnificent 7 member.
Mag 7 / hyperscaler AI capex projected at ~$725B in 2026 (JPMorgan), up from ~$450B in 2025.

data_points.metric: Meta investment in Scale AI (49% non-voting stake)
Meta acquisition of Manus (blocked by China)
Big Five acquisition count YTD 2026 (decade low)
